text:
There was a wise old owl that could see into the far future.

One day an eagle swooped down close to his nest. The wise old owl knew what eagles liked to eat. He quietly flew away, leaving a seed.

The egg hatched. The young eagle grew big.

When he was five years old, the wise old owl came back.

"I've come a little further," he said.

"You'll always come."

"Even I can't steal that."

"You'll find plenty."

They flew north. They talked.

After lots of flying, the eagle learned all sorts of things.

When old age crept near, the eagle knew his last flights would be long.

That night, a tired owl tucked him into sleep.

The next morning, an eagle found him.

"That's the last you'll eat," he said.

The wise old owl drifted away, leaving tales.<|endoftext|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|>
